zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Saville於1439年出生在英國越可爾艾藍德時，他的父親Sir John Saville30歲，母親Anne Gascoigne34歲。他於1463年與Ann de Standisfield在英國越可爾艾藍德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1481年在他的家鄉享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; Yorkshire): habitational name from a place in northern France of which the identity is not clear, though it is probably Sainville in Eure-et-Loire, so called from Old French saisne ‘Saxon’ + ville ‘settlement’. Compare Savel , Savell , and Savelle .

variants.name: Savel
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Šavel): from the Biblical personal name Šavel, Hebrew Shaul (see Saul ). It may be a nickname for a godless person, from the archaic word šavel, which is derived from the Biblical name.

Slovenian (Šavel): probably from an old spelling of the Biblical personal name Savel (see Saul , compare 2 above). Alternatively, perhaps a nickname or a topographic name from an old vernacular spelling of the same plant name as in 4 below.
Americanized form of Slovenian Šavelj: probably a nickname or a topographic name from the plant name š(č)avelj ‘sorrel (Rumex acetosa)’.

variants.name: Savelle
meanings: English: variant of   Saville  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Saffell
meanings: English (Essex): from the Middle English personal name Safugel (Old Norse sǽfogl ‘sea-bird, especially the cormorant’), not recorded as a personal name in Scandinavia, but common in England after the Norman Conquest. In some cases with voicing of intervocalic /f/ to /v/, there may have been confusion with Saville .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Saville was born in 1439, in Elland, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir John Saville, was 30 and his mother, Anne Gascoigne, was 34. He married Ann de Standisfield in 1463, in Elland, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1481, in his hometown, at the age of 42.

Il a épousé Ann de Standisfield en 1463, à Elland, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1481, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Savilleが1439年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエランドで生まれた時，父Sir John Savilleは30歳，母Anne Gascoigneは34歳であった。1463年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエランドでAnn de Standisfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1481年に故郷で42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Saville(이)가 1439년 영국 요르크실르 엘랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Saville(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Gascoigne(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1463년 영국 요르크실르 엘랜드에서 Ann de Standisfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1481년 그의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas de Saville родился в 1439 г. в местности элланд, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Saville было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Gascoigne, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann de Standisfield в 1463 г. в местности элланд, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1481 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.
